reasoning: The informal statement says "if n is an integer greater than 1, then n does not divide 2^n - 1". The Lean uses `n : ℕ` with `hn : n > 1`, which is equivalent to "integer greater than 1" since integers > 1 correspond exactly to natural numbers > 1. The conclusion `¬((n : ℤ) ∣ 2^n - 1)` correctly coerces n to an integer and states non-divisibility of 2^n - 1 (which is elaborated in ℤ due to the left side of ∣). The quantifier structure, hypotheses, and conclusion all match the informal claim.
